heading: Appeal to the Patent Trial and Appeal Board
label: 134 Appeal to the Patent Trial and Appeal Board
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on Title 35, U.S.C., 1946 ed., § 57 (R.S. 4909 amended (1) , , 1336, (2) , ).Mar. 2, 1927, ch. 273, § 544 Stat. 1335Aug. 5, 1939, ch. 451, § 253 Stat. 1212Reference to reissues is omitted in view of the general provision in section 251. Minor changes in language are made.

Amendments2011—, amended section catchline generally. Prior to amendment, section catchline read as follows: “Appeal to the Board of Patent Appeals and Interferences”.Pub. L. 112–29, § 3(j)(3)Subsec. (a). , substituted “Patent Trial and Appeal Board” for “Board of Patent Appeals and Interferences”.Pub. L. 112–29, § 3(j)(1)Subsec. (b). , substituted “a reexamination” for “any reexamination proceeding”.Pub. L. 112–29, § 7(b)(1), substituted “Patent Trial and Appeal Board” for “Board of Patent Appeals and Interferences”.Pub. L. 112–29, § 3(j)(1)Subsec. (c). , struck out subsec. (c). Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “A third-party requester in an inter partes proceeding may appeal to the Board of Patent Appeals and Interferences from the final decision of the primary examiner favorable to the patentability of any original or proposed amended or new claim of a patent, having once paid the fee for such appeal.”Pub. L. 112–29, § 7(b)(2)2002—Subsecs. (a), (b). , substituted “primary examiner” for “administrative patent judge”.Pub. L. 107–273, § 13202(b)(1)Subsec. (c). , substituted “primary examiner” for “administrative patent judge”.Pub. L. 107–273, § 13202(b)(1), struck out at end “The third-party requester may not appeal the decision of the Board of Patent Appeals and Interferences.”Pub. L. 107–273, § 13106(b)1999— reenacted section catchline without change and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “An applicant for a patent, any of whose claims has been twice rejected, may appeal from the decision of the primary examiner to the Board of Patent Appeals and Interferences, having once paid the fee for such appeal.”Pub. L. 106–1131984— substituted “Patent Appeals and Interferences” for “Appeals” in section catchline and text.Pub. L. 98–622

L. 112–29section 7(e) of Pub. L. 112–29section 6 of this titleSept. 16, 2011

children.heading: .—Patent Applicant
children.label: a .—Patent Applicant
children.text: An applicant for a patent, any of whose claims has been twice rejected, may appeal from the decision of the primary examiner to the Patent Trial and Appeal Board, having once paid the fee for such appeal.

children.heading: .—Patent Owner
children.label: b .—Patent Owner
children.text: A patent owner in a reexamination may appeal from the final rejection of any claim by the primary examiner to the Patent Trial and Appeal Board, having once paid the fee for such appeal.
